title: Proposition: Support a more open Containerfile in addition to Dockerfile
body:
Hi everyone,

Not sure if this issue is a good one for `buildah` or for podman proper repo. Please let me know if I should open this in libpod instead. 

In the spirit of removing dependencies on Docker and fostering a spirit of more a open environment for building OCI-compatible container images, have we given any thought to automatically supporting other file names for container build instructions other than `Dockerfile`?  If not, I would suggest using `Containerfile` as a recognized file name for building images.   I gave thought to `Podfile` or `Podmanfile`, but I am tempted to think that would ultimately create the same confusion among users and tie the containers back to single project. I wouldn't want users thinking that they couldn't build a Podmanfile, if they aren't using Podman, or to think we are creating a new format for container images.  I think supporting both `Dockerfile` and `Containerfile` file names may  be a step in the right direction for backward compatibility with Docker-based projects as well as be good to seperate the concept of OCI container images away from Docker and make it more general. 

Just my two cents. Thoughts?

comments.body:
I like the thought and will be interested to see what others think.  I'll keep this open for now, but I think a better place to ask would be the Podman mailing list.  Would you mind mailing the above there too please?  

If you're not already on that list, you can find signup directions [here](https://podman.io/community)

Thanks!

Sounds like a good idea, I also like the "Containerfile" - rather than something like Podmanfile.

Currently using a Makefile to build it, where the `$(DOCKER)` can be replaced with `sudo podman`.

```make
all: build

build:
	$(DOCKER) build -t $(IMAGE_NAME):$(IMAGE_TAG) .
```

Renaming the "Dockerfile" would be a natural fit for this...

Wonder what a better name for the make variable would be ?
@afbjorklund  -- since it's a variable,  could  it be `CONTAINER_BUILD_INTERFACE`  or just `CONTAINER_INTERFACE` ?

> @afbjorklund -- since it's a variable, could it be `CONTAINER_BUILD_INTERFACE` or just `CONTAINER_INTERFACE` ?

Well, those are better than `$(CONTAINER)`. But maybe I will just go with BUILD instead.

i.e. put the abstraction at "docker build" rather than just docker, with all the connotations.
I'm also going to open an issue, potentially a PR on podman-compose to try and get a similar functionality there:  `container-compose.yml`
I also opened a PR against `podman-compose`:  https://github.com/containers/podman-compose/pull/46
